title: Account setup revamp
summary: Does a shorter setup flow increase wizard completion? No, it didn't.
hypothesis: This experiment creates a new account setup experiment for 50% of new signups. This account setup flow is designed to be shorter, more aesthetically pleasing, and delightful to use. We are also adding a new step prompting the user to confirm their branding, which is optional. We believe this will lead to earlier personalization and better overall activation.
results: Treatment failed to meaningfully outperform control. Decision: ship control group. New branding experience showed a 10%+ conversion increase vs. legacy branding flow.
learning: Mobile responsiveness was a critical issue — shrunken components and partially hidden navigation made it hard to progress on mobile. The new branding experience showed promise and has been shipped to all users independently.
notes: 5 design screenshots attached in the Slack thread. Industry data no longer collected as a step in setup — gathered via BuiltWith in background and classified by DS model (CompanyWebsiteAnalysis table).
treatment_description: New /setup-account flow with streamlined steps and brand onboarding experience
